mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive 60-day return in > 50% of cases over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO above trend yields negative 20-day returns in > 55% of observations during early-cycle expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which improves near-term corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 5 percent, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 5.0 fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with positive outcomes exceeding 50% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms healthy labor market expansion without slack, supporting consumer spending and positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side bottlenecks and reduced output, leading to immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances.
